highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.126 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a dynamic model picker that lists models from your gateway's /v1/models endpoint when using a custom ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, making it easier to switch models when running behind a compatible API gateway. A new `claude project purge [path]` command lets you cleanly wipe all Claude Code state for a project — including transcripts, tasks, file history, and config entries — which is handy for starting fresh. Update via `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@latest` to get these improvements.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.122 Released — Bedrock Tier Selection & PR URL Resume
highlights.summary: This release introduces the `ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_SERVICE_TIER` environment variable, letting AWS Bedrock users select between `default`, `flex`, or `priority` service tiers to control throughput and cost. You can now paste a pull request URL directly into the `/resume` search box to instantly find and restore the session that originally created that PR, saving time hunting through session history. If you use Claude Code through AWS Bedrock, this update is especially worth picking up.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.121 Released — alwaysLoad for MCP & Plugin Pruning
highlights.summary: A new `alwaysLoad` option in MCP server config ensures that all tools from a specified server are always available without going through tool-search deferral, which reduces latency for frequently used MCP tools. The `claude plugin prune` command removes orphaned auto-installed plugin dependencies, and `plugin uninstall --prune` cascades the cleanup automatically, keeping your environment tidy. If you rely heavily on MCP servers in your workflow, setting `alwaysLoad: true` on your core servers is an immediate win.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family, bringing enhanced capabilities for complex reasoning and coding tasks. This new model is likely to be accessible through Claude Code's model picker, giving developers access to more powerful intelligence for challenging multi-step coding problems. Keep an eye on the Anthropic blog for benchmark details and pricing to decide when to route your hardest tasks to Opus 4.7.

highlights.title: Who Owns the Code Claude Code Wrote?
highlights.summary: This legal analysis explores the copyright ownership questions that arise when AI tools like Claude Code generate substantial portions of your codebase, a topic increasingly relevant as agentic coding sessions become longer and more autonomous. The piece examines how current law may treat AI-assisted code differently depending on the degree of human creative input involved. Developers shipping products built heavily with Claude Code should read this to understand the intellectual property landscape before it affects them.

highlights.title: Claude Code: Zero to One — MCP, SubAgents, Hooks, Images, Context & Background Tasks (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Chinese-language Bilibili tutorial walks through Claude Code from initial setup all the way to advanced topics including MCP integration, sub-agent orchestration, hooks, image handling, context management, and running background tasks. Timestamped sections let you jump directly to the topic you need, making it a practical reference rather than a watch-once video. Even if you don't read Chinese, the structured timestamp outline in the description is a great map of Claude Code's feature surface.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a fast, focused introduction to Claude Code's most important features, designed to get you productive within half an hour. It's a great option if you want a time-boxed overview rather than a multi-hour deep dive, covering the core workflows developers use daily. Pair it with the official docs to reinforce what you learn and fill any gaps.

highlights.title: DeepClaude — Claude Code Agent Loop with DeepSeek V4 Pro
highlights.summary: DeepClaude is an open-source project that wires Claude Code's agent loop together with DeepSeek V4 Pro, letting you experiment with an alternative model backend inside a familiar Claude Code-style interface. This is useful for developers who want to reduce API costs or compare output quality between models on the same tasks. Check the repo's README for setup instructions and be aware this is an experimental community tool, not an official Anthropic product.

tips_of_the_day: Use `claude project purge [path]` to reset a project's Claude Code state completely before starting a major refactor — it clears transcripts, tasks, and config entries so you get a clean slate without old context bleeding in.

If you use MCP servers for tools you call in nearly every session (like a database schema inspector or a test runner), add `"alwaysLoad": true` to those server entries in your MCP config so their tools are instantly available without waiting for tool-search deferral.

When resuming work on a pull request, paste the PR URL directly into Claude Code's `/resume` search box instead of scrolling through sessions by date — it pinpoints the exact session that created the PR in seconds.
